description_long: This guard rail consists of two robust vertical posts connected by a horizontal top rail and a lower cross-bracing system. The posts are octagonal with decorative caps, providing both structural support and an aesthetic touch. The central section features a geometric X-brace with a hexagonal ring at the intersection, adding rigidity and visual interest. The entire structure is coated in a dark green finish, typical of urban park or sidewalk barriers. The rail appears to be made of metal, likely steel, and is designed to prevent pedestrian or vehicular passage while allowing visibility. The ends of the posts are blunt and squared, with no visible mounting hardware or reflective markings.
description: A dark green metal guard rail with octagonal posts, X-bracing, and a central hexagonal ring, designed for urban environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full X-brace, hexagonal ring, and both octagonal posts with caps.
description_view_1: Side view showing the narrow profile of a single post and the thickness of the rail.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, displaying the X-brace and hexagonal ring.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile of a single post.
